info.name: Dialist API
info.description:
Full Dialist API collection — all 213+ endpoints across Marketplace, Networks, User, Auth, and Admin modules.

## Quick Start
1. Import both this collection AND the `Dialist-LocalDev.postman_environment.json` file
2. Select **Dialist - Local Dev** as the active environment
3. Start the server: `npm run dev` (listens on port 5050)
4. Run `Debug > Get All Mock Users` to see available personas
5. Change `{{mockUser}}` in the environment to switch testing persona
6. Use Collection Runner to run folders in order for end-to-end flows

## Auth
Controlled by the `authMode` environment variable:

**Mode 1 — Mock (default, `authMode = mock`)**
Injects `x-test-user: {{mockUser}}` header — no Clerk account needed.
Change `{{mockUser}}` to switch persona:
- `buyer_us_complete` — fully onboarded US buyer
- `merchant_approved` — approved US seller
- `merchant_approved_ca` — approved Canadian seller
- `new_user_us` — fresh US user (test onboarding)
- `new_user_ca` — fresh Canadian user
- `merchant_pending` / `merchant_provisioning` / `merchant_rejected`

**Mode 2 — Real Clerk JWT (`authMode = jwt`)**
Injects `Authorization: Bearer {{token}}` header for a real Clerk user.
Supports: `{{token}}`, `{{buyerToken}}`, or `{{sellerToken}}`
How to use:
1. Open the app in the browser, sign in as the real user
2. Open DevTools → Network tab → any API request → copy the `Authorization` header value (without the `Bearer ` prefix)
3a. For general token: paste into `token` env variable
3b. For buyer requests: paste into `buyerToken` env variable + set `tokenRole = buyer`
3c. For seller requests: paste into `sellerToken` env variable + set `tokenRole = seller`
4. Set `authMode` to `jwt`
Note: Tokens valid ~12 hours — refresh from DevTools when they expire.

## ID Chaining
Create requests auto-save response IDs to environment variables:
`{{listingId}}`, `{{orderId}}`, `{{offerId}}`, `{{connectionId}}`, `{{groupId}}`, etc.
Subsequent requests reference these automatically.

exec: // Runs before every request in this collection.
// Auth mode is controlled by the 'authMode' environment variable:
// Support for tokenRole: can use 'token', 'buyerToken', or 'sellerToken'
if (!selectedToken) console.warn('[Auth] tokenRole=buyer but buyerToken is empty.');
if (!selectedToken) console.warn('[Auth] tokenRole=seller but sellerToken is empty.');
if (!selectedToken) console.warn('[Auth] authMode=jwt but token env var is empty. Set token in environment.');
// Remove mock header so it does not leak alongside JWT
// --- Mock user auth (local dev, no Clerk required) --------
// Runs after every request in this collection.
// Only check Content-Type on non-empty responses

item.name: 🔧 Debug
item.description: Debug endpoints — inspect mock users, server health, and spec.

item.name: 👤 User — Profile
item.description:
Current user profile, avatar, status, verification, wishlist.
Set {{mockUser}} in environment to control which user you're acting as.

item.name: 🛍️ Marketplace — User Onboarding
item.description: Marketplace onboarding flow: buyer/dealer intent selection with Finix auto-start for dealers

item.name: 1. GET Status (Pre-Marketplace)
item.description:
Check marketplace onboarding status. Shows pre-populated data from Networks if completed.

Use mock user: buyer_us_complete (has Networks done)

item.name: 2. PATCH Complete (Buyer Intent)
item.description: Complete marketplace onboarding as BUYER. No merchant onboarding auto-start.

exec: pm.test('No merchant onboarding started for buyer', () => {

item.name: 3. PATCH Complete (Dealer Intent + Finix Auto-Start)
item.description:
Complete marketplace onboarding as DEALER. Auto-starts Finix merchant onboarding and returns form link.

Use mock user: buyer_us_complete

item.name: 4. GET Status (After Completion)
item.description: Verify marketplace onboarding is now completed. Should show completed status and user_type.

item.name: 🏷️ Networks — Listings
item.description:
Create, update, publish, and manage Networks listings.
IMPORTANT: Use `merchant_approved` as mockUser when creating listings.

item.name: 💰 Networks — Offers
item.description:
Send and manage offers on listings.
Set mockUser to `buyer_us_complete` to send; `merchant_approved` to receive.

request.description: Both buyer AND seller must call this endpoint. Order completes when both confirm.

item.name: Get My Incoming Connections
item.description: Get pending incoming connection requests (people wanting to connect with me) - includes requester info

item.name: Get My Outgoing Connections
item.description: Get pending outgoing connection requests (people I asked to connect with)

item.name: Get My Accepted Connections
item.description: Get my accepted/active connections

item.name: Get Network Onboarding Status
item.description: Check current Networks onboarding status for the authenticated user.

exec: pm.test('Response contains current onboarding step confirmations', () => {

item.name: Complete Network Onboarding (Atomic)
item.description: Complete Networks onboarding atomically with profile, location, and avatar (no payment payload).

item.name: Complete Network Onboarding (Upload Avatar)
item.description: Complete Networks onboarding with uploaded avatar URL (no payment payload).

exec: pm.test('Onboarding complete with upload avatar - 200 OK', () => {

item.name: 🏪 Marketplace — Listings
item.description:
Use mockUser = `merchant_approved` for create/update/delete.
Use `buyer_us_complete` for browsing.

item.name: 🛒 Marketplace — Orders (Finix Payments)
item.description:
Full Finix payment flow:
1. Reserve → get fraud_session_id
2. Tokenize → get Finix config for CardTokenForm
3. Pay (with payment_token from Finix.js)
4. Refund

Use mockUser = `buyer_us_complete` as buyer.

item.name: 🏢 Marketplace — Merchant Onboarding
item.description:
Finix merchant onboarding flow.
Use mockUser = `new_user_us` or `merchant_pending`.

item.name: 1. POST Onboard (Create/Reuse Finix Form)
item.description:
Start or continue merchant onboarding with Finix.
- First call: creates new form, returns link
- Subsequent calls: reuses existing form (status 200), or creates new link if expired

Prereq: Marketplace onboarding completed as dealer
Mock users: buyer_us_complete (new form) / merchant_pending (reuse)

item.name: 2. GET Status (Merchant Verification State)
item.description:
Check merchant verification status and state.
- onboarding_state: PENDING | PROVISIONING | APPROVED | REJECTED | UPDATE_REQUESTED
- is_merchant: true only when APPROVED
- user_type: reflects merchant status

Try with: merchant_pending, merchant_approved, merchant_rejected

item.name: 3. POST Refresh Link (Expired Form)
item.description:
Regenerate an expired Finix form link for an existing merchant.
Only works if MerchantOnboarding record exists.

Mock user: merchant_pending (will generate new link)

exec: pm.test('Response has new onboarding_url and expires_at', () => {

item.name: 4. GET Profile (Merchant Profile & Status)
item.description:
Get full merchant profile including Finix verification details.

Response includes:
- is_merchant: true if APPROVED
- merchant: Finix form/state details
- user: user profile (name, email, etc.)

item.name: 🛡️ Admin
item.description: Admin endpoints — switch mockUser if needed (check with /debug/mock-users which users have admin role).

item.name: 🔗 Webhooks
item.description: Webhook receiver endpoints — these are called by external services (Finix, Clerk), not by the frontend.
